title: Repository State, References, and Revision Algebra · Command Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs repository state, references, and revision algebra from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the repository-state and revision-query proof. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for repository-state and revision-query proof after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
Resolve revision names and disambiguate refs, object IDs, paths, reflog selectors, and peel operators before an operation.
Construct inclusive, exclusive, symmetric-difference, ancestry, parent, and path-limited revision sets for an exact question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G78 is a review in a disposable repository. the audit mismatch is confined to commits reachable from exactly one reviewed tip. The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip. Competency probe: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

The learner must predict left and right markers expose exclusive ancestry without moving a reference, run git rev-list --left-right --ancestry-path release...topic -- src/policy.ts, explain two right-only policy commits and one left-only release fix define the review set, test this changed condition: replace topic with topic^{tree} and reject the query because a tree has no commit ancestry, and preserve this recovery boundary: read-only query; retain refs/audit/before-query at the reviewed release tip. Competency probe: Distinguish direct refs, symbolic refs, pseudorefs, remote-tracking refs, packed refs, and detached HEAD using plumbing evidence.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Every ref is a loose text file under .git/refs.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph for the review Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref case
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology for the review Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref case
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation for the review Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref case
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path for the review Gita Ref Symbolic Pseudoref case
